frameworks.name: membrane polarization
frameworks.one_line: tDCS modulates neuronal membrane potential to alter cortical excitability.

frameworks.name: dopaminergic neuromodulation
frameworks.one_line: Prior cathodal tDCS work linked to striatal extracellular dopamine increases.

core_question: How does anodal versus cathodal transcranial direct current stimulation alter single-unit firing in the rat primary motor cortex?
key_assumptions: tDCS polarity shifts neuronal membrane potential
Extracellular single-unit firing rate indexes cortical excitability
Anesthetized rat M1 is a valid preparation for tDCS effects
key_findings: Both anodal and cathodal tDCS increased spike firing rate in M1
Cathodal tDCS produced a larger and longer-lasting firing increase than anodal
interpretations: Cathodal tDCS enhances cortical neuronal excitability at the single-unit level
tDCS leaves an immediate electrophysiological signature in M1 firing
